context: Tests whether the agent collects SMS marketing consent correctly at checkout: unchecked checkbox by default, full audit trail storage, phone normalization to E.164, separation of marketing vs transactional consent types, and GDPR-ready export capability.

checklist.name: Checkbox not pre-checked
checklist.description: The opt-in checkbox is explicitly set to unchecked by default (e.g., defaultChecked={false} or checked={false} or equivalent) — NOT left to browser defaults or set to true

checklist.name: Consent timestamp stored
checklist.description: The consent record includes a timestamp field (e.g., consentGivenAt, created_at, or similar) capturing when consent was given

checklist.name: IP address stored
checklist.description: The consent record stores the user's IP address

checklist.name: Consent source stored
checklist.description: The consent record stores how/where consent was collected (e.g., 'checkout', 'popup', 'keyword', or equivalent source field)

checklist.name: Consent type stored
checklist.description: The consent record distinguishes between 'marketing' and 'transactional' consent types — both types are represented as distinct values

checklist.name: E.164 phone format
checklist.description: Phone numbers are normalized to E.164 format (starting with '+' followed by country code and number, e.g. +12125551234) before being stored

checklist.name: Marketing vs transactional separation
checklist.description: Marketing consent and transactional consent are stored or queried as separate types — the implementation does NOT conflate them into a single boolean flag

checklist.name: STOP language in opt-in UI
checklist.description: The opt-in UI or consent language includes a reference to replying STOP (or similar keyword) to unsubscribe

checklist.name: GDPR export includes required fields
checklist.description: The consent export or audit trail includes at minimum: phone, consentType, consentSource, consentGivenAt, and ipAddress fields

checklist.name: revokedAt in GDPR export
checklist.description: The consent export includes a revocation timestamp field (revokedAt or equivalent) that can be null when consent is still active

checklist.name: Privacy policy linked
checklist.description: The opt-in UI includes a link or reference to a privacy policy
